title: Add drag-and-drop sorting for links
description: Allow users to reorder links by dragging them. Adds sortOrder column to schema, reorder API endpoint, and drag-and-drop UI with visual feedback.

title: Documentation: Google Integration Setup
description:
## Google Integration Setup Guide

### Prerequisites
- A Google account (personal or work)
- Access to Google Cloud Console

### Step 1: Create a Google Cloud Project
1. Go to https://console.cloud.google.com/
2. Click the project dropdown (top left) → **New Project**
3. Name your project (e.g., "Active Tabs") and click **Create**
4. Wait for the project to be created, then select it

### Step 2: Enable Required APIs
1. Go to **APIs & Services** → **Library**
2. Search for and enable these APIs:
   - **Google Docs API**
   - **Google Sheets API**
   - **Google Drive API**

### Step 3: Configure OAuth Consent Screen
1. Go to **APIs & Services** → **OAuth consent screen**
2. Select **External** (or Internal if using Google Workspace)
3. Click **Create**
4. Fill in required fields:
   - App name: "Active Tabs"
   - User support email: Your email
   - Developer contact: Your email
5. Click **Save and Continue**
6. On **Scopes** page, click **Add or Remove Scopes** and add:
   - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.metadata.readonly`
   - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/documents.readonly`
   - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/spreadsheets.readonly`
7. Click **Save and Continue**
8. On **Test users** page, add your email address
9. Click **Save and Continue**

### Step 4: Create OAuth Credentials
1. Go to **APIs & Services** → **Credentials**
2. Click **Create Credentials** → **OAuth client ID**
3. Application type: **Web application**
4. Name: "Active Tabs"
5. Under **Authorized redirect URIs**, add:
   ```
   http://localhost:5173/api/auth/google/callback
   ```
6. Click **Create**
7. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret**

### Step 5: Configure Active Tabs
1. Open Active Tabs and go to **Settings**
2. Find **Google** in the Connected Services list
3. Click **Configure**
4. Paste your **Client ID** and **Client Secret**
5. Click **Save Configuration**

### Step 6: Connect Your Google Account
1. After saving the config, click **Connect with Google**
2. A popup will open for Google authentication
3. Select your Google account and grant permissions
4. The popup will show "Connected successfully!" and auto-close
5. The Settings page will refresh showing Google as connected

### What Works
- **Google Docs** (`docs.google.com/document/d/...`) → Shows document title
- **Google Sheets** (`docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/...`) → Shows spreadsheet title

### Troubleshooting
- **"Google OAuth is not configured"**: Make sure you saved the Client ID and Secret first
- **Popup blocked**: Allow popups for localhost:5173
- **"Access denied"**: Add your email to Test Users in OAuth consent screen
- **Token expired**: Tokens auto-refresh. If issues persist, disconnect and reconnect.

### Notes
- Tokens are stored encrypted locally and auto-refresh when expired
- You can use a personal Google account to create the OAuth app, then authenticate with any account (personal or work)

title: MCP Notion lazy-connect fails after server restart
description: When trying to lazy-connect with persisted tokens, the MCP SDK throws 'Either provider.prepareTokenRequest() or authorizationCode is required' instead of using the stored OAuth tokens.

title: Phase 2: Core Link Management
description:
Implement basic link CRUD and display functionality.

## Tasks
- Create Link model with fields: url, title, description, createdAt, clickCount, lastClickedAt, pinned
- Build link creation form with URL input
- Implement link list view with sorting (pinned first, then by frequency/recency)
- Add click tracking (increment count, update lastClickedAt)
- Implement link editing and deletion
- Add pinning/unpinning functionality

title: Phase 5: Credential Management
description:
Secure storage for OAuth tokens and API keys.

## Tasks
- Create encrypted credential storage (using node crypto)
- Build credential management UI
- Implement OAuth flows for:
  - Google (Docs, Sheets, Drive)
  - Slack
  - GitHub
  - Linear
  - Notion
- Add personal access token storage for services that support it
- Create credential refresh/rotation logic
- Build connection status indicators

title: Show loading indicator during auto-summarization
description: When metadata refresh triggers auto-summarization, show a loading state so the user knows what's happening

title: MCP bridge for Notion integration
description:
MCP bridge for Notion integration - COMPLETED

Implemented full MCP OAuth flow for Notion:
- OAuth provider with token persistence to SQLite
- Lazy connection with automatic reconnect
- notion-fetch tool for retrieving page content
- Content extraction for summaries (title, description, full text)

Files:
- src/lib/server/mcp-notion.ts - MCP client and OAuth handling
- src/routes/api/auth/notion-mcp/* - OAuth endpoints

title: Linear summarization returns confused LLM response
description: Linear content fetcher may be returning empty/null content, causing the LLM to hallucinate about not being able to access URLs

title: Detect duplicate links
description: When adding a new link, check if the URL already exists in the database. Show a warning or prevent adding duplicates. Could also offer to navigate to the existing link instead.

title: Display click/usage counter on links
description:
Show usage statistics for each link in the list.

- Display click count
- Optionally show last clicked time (e.g., '3 clicks, last used 2h ago')
- Update counter when link is clicked

title: Phase 7: Polish & UX
description:
Refine the user experience and add quality-of-life features.

## Tasks
- Add keyboard shortcuts (quick search, navigation)
- Implement drag-and-drop reordering
- Add bulk operations (tag multiple, delete multiple)
- Create import/export functionality
- Add dark mode support
- Optimize for performance with many links
- Add browser extension for quick-add (stretch goal)

title: Active Tabs - Link Management App
description:
A local web app for managing, organizing, and intelligently summarizing browser links/tabs.

## Goals
- Replace browser tab hoarding with an organized link management system
- Smart link descriptions via content fetching and LLM summaries
- Track usage patterns to surface frequently-used links
- Support authenticated services (Slack, Google, GitHub, etc.)

## Key Features
1. Link storage with descriptions and tags
2. Click frequency tracking with smart ordering
3. Pin important links
4. Full-text search
5. Auto-generate descriptions from page content
6. LLM-powered summaries via Ollama
7. OAuth/token storage for authenticated services

title: GitHub favicon invisible in dark mode
description: The GitHub favicon SVG uses fill=#24292E (dark color) which is invisible on dark backgrounds. Fixed by adding dark:invert CSS class to invert the favicon in dark mode.

title: Phase 4: Content Fetching & Auto-Description
description:
Fetch page content and extract titles/descriptions automatically.

## Tasks
- Create content fetcher service with URL parsing
- Implement title extraction from HTML (og:title, title tag, h1)
- Add description extraction (og:description, meta description)
- Build service-specific extractors:
  - Google Docs/Sheets (via title in page)
  - GitHub PRs/Issues (via API or page scraping)
  - Linear items
  - Notion pages
  - Slack messages (will need API)
- Create background job system for fetching
- Add manual refresh button for re-fetching content

title: Documentation: LLM Summaries Setup
description: Create user-facing documentation for setting up and using LLM summaries feature

title: Phase 1: Project Setup & Core Infrastructure
description:
Set up the foundational project structure and tooling.

## Tasks
- Initialize SvelteKit project with TypeScript
- Set up SQLite with Drizzle ORM
- Configure Tailwind CSS
- Create database schema for links, tags, and credentials
- Set up basic routing structure

title: Auto-summarize links when metadata is fetched
description: Automatically generate summaries when link metadata is loaded or refreshed, instead of requiring manual click

title: Phase 6: LLM Summaries via Ollama
description:
Integrate local LLM for intelligent content summarization.

## Completed
- ✅ Set up Ollama client integration (src/lib/server/ollama.ts)
- ✅ Create summary generation service with configurable models
- ✅ Build summary storage (cached in links table)
- ✅ Add expandable summary UI component on main page
- ✅ Implement summary regeneration (force refresh)
- ✅ Service-aware content fetching (Notion via MCP, GitHub, Linear APIs)
- ✅ Clean LLM preamble from summaries
- ✅ Handle timeouts gracefully (60s timeout)

## Remaining
- Add model selection UI (allow different Ollama models)
- Create summary queue for batch processing
- Rate limiting for bulk operations

## Technical Notes
- Ollama runs on localhost:11434 by default
- Summaries are 2-3 sentences, stored in links.summary column
- Service-specific content fetching uses MCP for Notion, GraphQL for Linear, REST for GitHub
- Prompt engineered to avoid preamble; cleanSummary() strips remaining patterns

title: Documentation: Slack Integration Setup
description:
## Slack Integration Setup Guide

### Prerequisites
- A Slack workspace where you have permission to create apps

### Step 1: Create a Slack App
1. Go to https://api.slack.com/apps
2. Click **Create New App**
3. Choose **From scratch**
4. Name your app (e.g., "Active Tabs") and select your workspace
5. Click **Create App**

### Step 2: Configure OAuth Scopes
1. In the left sidebar, click **OAuth & Permissions**
2. Scroll to **User Token Scopes** and add:
   - `channels:read` - View basic info about public channels
   - `channels:history` - View messages in public channels
   - `groups:read` - View basic info about private channels (optional)
   - `groups:history` - View messages in private channels (optional)

**Note:** Use User Token Scopes, not Bot Token Scopes. The User OAuth Token can read messages from any public channel you have access to without the bot needing to be added to each channel.

### Step 3: Install the App
1. Scroll to the top of the **OAuth & Permissions** page
2. Click **Install to Workspace**
3. Review the permissions and click **Allow**

### Step 4: Copy the User OAuth Token
1. After installation, you'll see **OAuth Tokens for Your Workspace**
2. Copy the **User OAuth Token** (starts with `xoxp-`)
   - **Important:** Use the User token, NOT the Bot User OAuth Token

### Step 5: Add Token to Active Tabs
1. Open Active Tabs and go to **Settings**
2. Find **Slack** in the Connected Services list
3. Click **Connect** and paste your User OAuth Token
4. Click **Save Token**

### What Works
- **Channel links** (`slack.com/archives/C12345678`) → Shows channel name and description
- **Message links** (`slack.com/archives/C12345678/p1234567890`) → Shows message preview with channel name

### Troubleshooting
- **"not_in_channel" error**: You're using a Bot token. Switch to the User OAuth Token.
- **No message preview**: Make sure the URL contains `/p` followed by the message timestamp.

title: Phase 3: Tags & Search
description:
Add categorization and search capabilities.

## Tasks
- Create Tag model and link-tag relationship
- Build tag management UI (create, edit, delete tags)
- Add tag assignment to links
- Implement full-text search across titles, descriptions, URLs, and tags
- Add tag filtering in the UI
- Build search results view

title: Slack favicon invisible in dark mode
description: The Slack favicon likely has visibility issues on dark backgrounds, similar to the GitHub favicon issue.

title: Notion page summarization fails - uses HTTP fetch instead of MCP
description: The summarize endpoint uses a simple HTTP fetch (in ollama.ts fetchPageContent) which doesn't work for Notion pages since they require JavaScript. Need to use the MCP integration (fetchNotionPageViaMCP) to get actual content for summarization.

title: Notion MCP Integration
description:
Implemented Notion integration using MCP (Model Context Protocol) instead of internal integration tokens.

## What Changed
- Added MCP client in src/lib/server/mcp-notion.ts using @modelcontextprotocol/sdk
- OAuth flow via /api/auth/notion-mcp/* endpoints
- Tokens persisted to SQLite database
- Lazy connection on first use
- Removed PAT-based Notion integration from SUPPORTED_SERVICES
- Notion now appears as a single OAuth-based service in Settings

## Key Files
- src/lib/server/mcp-notion.ts - MCP client with OAuth provider
- src/lib/server/service-fetchers.ts - fetchNotionMetadata uses MCP
- src/routes/api/auth/notion-mcp/* - OAuth endpoints
- src/routes/settings/+page.svelte - Notion in Connected Services list

## Benefits
- No need to create internal integrations
- Access to all pages user can see (not just shared ones)
- Simpler setup flow (just OAuth)

title: Add pin/unpin toggle button to link list
description:
Add a clickable pin button directly in the link list so users can quickly pin/unpin links without opening the edit page.

- Show pin icon (📌) that's clickable
- Toggle pinned state via form action
- Visual feedback for pinned vs unpinned state
